skill.name: template-skill
skill.description: A minimal starter template for writing a custom AI skill with frontmatter and an instruction placeholder.
skill.summary: Start a custom AI skill from a simple SKILL.md template.
skill.author: Cam10001110101

security_audit.summary: The duplicate static findings for a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:3 are false positives. The file contains placeholder description text only, with no cryptography, executable code, network access, or prompt injection attempt found.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis reported a weak cryptographic algorithm twice at SKILL.md:3, but the line is only placeholder description text. No evidence found of DES, MD5, SHA1, encryption code, or security-sensitive logic.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The only referenced line is a plain description field, and the scanned skill has no code or cryptographic operation. Both static hits point to the same non-executable placeholder text.

content.value_statement: New skill authors need a clear starting file before adding domain instructions. This template provides the smallest valid structure for drafting a custom AI skill.
actual_capabilities: Provides a minimal SKILL.md file for a new skill.
Includes frontmatter fields for the skill name and description.
Marks the location where custom instructions should be added.
Can be adapted for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code skill workflows.
Keeps the initial structure small for quick review and customization.
limitations: Does not include domain-specific instructions or examples.
Does not provide scripts, tests, assets, or helper files.
Requires the author to replace placeholder text before publishing.
Does not validate finished skill behavior by itself.

use_cases.title: Draft a First Skill
use_cases.description: Use the template as a starting point for a new custom skill file.

use_cases.title: Standardize Skill Skeletons
use_cases.description: Give contributors a consistent base file before they add task-specific instructions.

use_cases.title: Teach Skill Structure
use_cases.description: Show the basic frontmatter and instruction area used by a simple skill.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this template to draft a skill for summarizing meeting notes. Replace the placeholder description and add clear instructions.

prompt_templates.title: Add Practical Instructions
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this template into a documentation review skill. Include when to use it, what files to inspect, and how to format feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my completed version of this template for clarity, missing limitations, and marketplace readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Reusable Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt this template into a reusable skill for code migration planning. Define scope, safe boundaries, expected inputs, and output format.

output_examples.input: Create a first draft for a release note writing skill.
output_examples.output: A concise skill draft with a specific description, usage guidance, and instructions for producing release notes.

output_examples.input: Review whether my template is ready to publish.
output: A list of missing instruction details.
Suggested improvements for description, scope, and limitations.

best_practices: Replace all placeholder text before publishing or sharing the skill.
Describe exactly when the skill should be used and when it should not be used.
Keep instructions specific, testable, and aligned with the intended workflow.
anti_patterns: Publishing the template without adding real task instructions.
Using broad descriptions that do not identify the skill purpose.
Adding hidden instructions that override user or system policies.

faq.question: What does this skill provide?
faq.answer: It provides a minimal SKILL.md starter file with frontmatter and a place for custom instructions.

faq.question: Is this a complete production skill?
faq.answer: No. It is a starter template that must be customized for a specific task.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it run scripts or access external services?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed file contains no scripts, network calls, or external command usage.

faq.question: What should I change first?
faq.answer: Replace the placeholder description with a clear summary of the skill purpose and usage conditions.

faq.question: Why was a security warning reported?
faq.answer: Static analysis flagged the placeholder description as a weak crypto pattern, but review found no cryptographic code.
